workLog.task: Issue #1975: /spec halt-block answer/evidence fields emit user-supplied free-text verbatim into git-committed artifacts (PR descriptions, session logs, tallies); a credential or PII in a Q3/Q4 answer is then disclosed for the life of the history (CWE-209, CWE-532)
workLog.outcome: Shipped the gate-free, reusable core: scripts/redact_secrets.py redacts token shapes (private keys, GitHub/Stripe/AWS/Slack tokens, JWTs, Bearer headers, emails, hex secrets >= 32) to [redacted: <reason>], with an include_hex=False mode so structured git-SHA fields are not corrupted. Added a .claude/rules/secret-redaction.md policy (redact before emit; do not redact SHA fields; state that halt blocks are durable) and generated its instruction mirrors. 16 pos/neg/edge/CLI tests. The spec.md Step 0/0.5 prose wiring is deferred: it edits a command (ADR-057 eval gate) and conflicts with the unmerged #2001 PR; it should batch with that merge.
workLog.evidence: pytest tests/test_redact_secrets.py -> 16 passed; CLI redacts the issue's example; install-parity OK

nextSteps: Open PR with Fixes #1975 (helper + rule core)
Follow-up: wire scripts/redact_secrets.py into spec.md Step 0/0.5 halt-block emit (ADR-057 eval; batch after #2001 merges) and into session-log writers
Follow-up: vendor-portability of scripts/redact_secrets.py (ship into the plugin lib) tracked under #2050
